name: Denmark (Splitflag)
description: The white Nordic cross of Denmark on a red field, in the swallow-tailed (split) form reserved for the state, navy and royal family.
history: The split version of the Dannebrog has been associated with the Danish Crown since at least the 15th century and was restricted to state and military use by royal decrees of 1696 and 1748. The current proportions were standardised in 1854.

features.symbolism: the Dannebrog cross, the world's oldest continuously used national flag design
